reasoning: Multiple sources confirm that a major conflict occurred between India and Pakistan in May 2025, sparked by an April 22 terror attack in Pahalgam. The kinetic phase of this conflict took place over four days, from May 6 to May 10, 2025. On May 10, a ceasefire mediated by the United States was announced. Post-conflict analyses from June, July, and August 2025 uniformly refer to the event as the “May 2025 India-Pakistan Conflict” or “Four Days in May.” Although there were reports of the ceasefire fraying or being violated shortly after it was signed, there is no evidence of Indian kinetic attacks extending into June, nor operations deep into Pakistan proper (Sindh, Baluchistan, Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa) during the month of June. Official statements confirm that fighting effectively halted on May 10 after India struck several Pakistani airfields. Given the retroactive consistency in naming this a purely May conflict and the absence of any reports indicating a resumption of strikes in June, the probability of an Indian kinetic attack in Pakistan proper in June 2025 is extremely low.
